temple.name: The Hindu Temple of Metropolitan Washington

history: This was the first temple in the DMV area. The temple started from a home in 1988 and later moved to its new building in 2007.
The objective of The Hindu Temple of Metropolitan Washington is to give genuine religious services in accordance with the Sastras to benefit individuals, families, and the community at large.
The mission also includes preserving and propagating Vedic, Sastra, Purana, and Hindu traditions, as well as instilling Sanatana Dharma ideals, culture, and heritage in future generations.
about.mission: To provide genuine religious services in accordance with the Sastras, benefiting individuals, families, and the community.
about.vision: To preserve Hindu traditions and instill Sanatana Dharma ideals, culture, and heritage in future generations across the DMV area.

timeline.text: Temple started from a home, serving the growing Hindu community in the DMV area.
Moved to the new building at 10001 Riggs Road, Adelphi, MD 20783.
Continuing to serve as a beacon of Sanatana Dharma for the metropolitan Washington community.

sanskar.name: Namkaran
sanskar.desc: On the tenth, eleventh, or twelfth day, this Sanskar is conducted with Mantra recitation. After completing this Sanskar, the child is given a name based on the 27 Nakshatras and the moon's position at the moment of birth.

sanskar.name: Annaprasnan
sanskar.desc: On the sixth month, when the child is given solid food for the first time, this sanskar is performed. Mantras are recited and various deities are offered oblations.

sanskar.name: Mundan
sanskar.desc: This Sanskar marks the first time a child's hair has been cut. After reaching the age of one year, the ceremony is held on an auspicious day to promote power, understanding, and longevity.

sanskar.name: Upnayan
sanskar.desc: The ceremony of wearing the sacred thread known as Yajnopaveetam. The sacred thread is ceremoniously worn when a male child reaches the age of five — this is a spiritual birth.

sanskar.name: Vivah
sanskar.desc: The beginning of one's life as a family. Vedic Hindu marriage is seen as sacramental, with one wife and one husband committing to each other for the rest of their lives before Agni.

sanskar.name: Anteshthi
sanskar.desc: The ceremonies related with funerals — last rites. When death is imminent, gold, tulsi leaf, and Ganga water are placed in the mouth. The eldest son usually administers the last rites.

other.name: Mata Ki Chowki & Bhajan Sandhya
other.desc: Devotional singing and prayers dedicated to Goddess Durga with bhajans and kirtans.

other.name: Sundar Kand Paath
other.desc: Recitation of Sundar Kand from Ramcharitmanas, dedicated to Lord Hanuman.

other.name: Rudra Abhishekam
other.desc: Sacred abhishekam ritual for Lord Shiva with Vedic mantras and offerings.

other.name: Satyanarayan Katha
other.desc: Puja and katha dedicated to Lord Vishnu for prosperity and blessings.

other.name: Griha Pravesh
other.desc: Housewarming ceremony to invoke blessings for a new home.

other.name: Sagai Samaroh / Engagement Ceremony
other.desc: Traditional engagement ceremony with rituals and blessings for the couple.

other.name: Sehra Bandhi Ceremony
other.desc: Pre-wedding ceremony where the groom is adorned with the sehra (wedding crown).

other.name: Wedding Ceremony
other.desc: Complete Vedic Hindu wedding ceremony with sacred fire and traditional rituals.

other.name: Shanti Paath
other.desc: Peace ceremony with Vedic chants for harmony, prosperity, and well-being.

other.name: Shraddha Karam
other.desc: Ancestral rites and ceremonies to honor departed souls.

other.name: Car Puja
other.desc: Vehicle blessing ceremony for safety and auspicious beginnings.

programs.title: Dollar-a-Day Program
programs.desc: A small daily contribution that makes a big difference. Just $1 a day — $365 a year — helps sustain our temple's daily operations, maintenance, and community programs.

programs.title: One-Time Donation
programs.desc: Make a one-time gift of any amount to support the temple. Every contribution, no matter the size, helps us serve the community.

programs.title: Recurring Donation
programs.desc: Set up a monthly or annual recurring donation to provide steady support for temple activities and operations.

programs.title: Sponsorships
programs.desc: Sponsor a specific event, puja, or community program. In-person and remote sponsorships are welcome.

forms.title: Check / Online Payment Form
forms.desc: For check or PayPal/credit card payments

donate.quote: No organization can continue without the support of the community. Please help us in any way you can — by providing time, money, or simply by joining and being with us.

membership.quote: We make a living by what we get, but we make a life by what we give
membership.programDescription: This program was initiated to help devotees support the day-to-day maintenance and operation of the Hindu Temple, providing religious programs to our community. Members commit to provide this support year after year. A monthly donation of $31 (approximately one dollar per day) or another specific amount will help sustain our temple's mission.
membership.memberRecognition: Name tags of all members are displayed in the Hindu Temple lobby for membership recognition and appreciation.
membership.taxDeductible: All donations are tax deductible to the extent allowed by law.

forms.title: Dollar-A-Day Membership Form
forms.description: Complete this form to join our Dollar-A-Day membership program with check or online payment options

forms.title: Dollar-A-Day Membership Notice
forms.description: Learn about our Dollar-A-Day membership program and its benefits to the temple community

forms.title: Auto Debit Authorization Form
forms.description: Set up automatic monthly debit for convenient recurring donations to support the temple

submissionSteps.title: Download and Complete
submissionSteps.desc: Download the appropriate form and fill it out completely.

submissionSteps.title: Submit Your Form
submissionSteps.desc: Give the completed form to Mrs. Khanna, Dr. Bajpai, or Hindu Temple Priests/Manager, or mail it to: Hindu Temple, 10001 Riggs Road, Adelphi, MD 20783

submissionSteps.title: Keep Us Updated
submissionSteps.desc: Please notify the temple of any changes to your contact or mailing address to ensure you receive your tax-deductible annual donation statement.

home.heroSubtitle: Preserving and propagating Vedic traditions, Sastra, Purana, and Hindu heritage — instilling Sanatana Dharma ideals in future generations.
home.shantMantra: Om, May all be happy, May all be free from illness, May all see what is auspicious, May no one suffer, Om Peace, Peace, Peace.
aboutText: This was the first temple in the DMV area. The temple started from a home in 1988 and later moved to its new building in 2007. The objective of The Hindu Temple of Metropolitan Washington is to give genuine religious services in accordance with the Sastras to benefit individuals, families, and the community at large.
The mission also includes preserving and propagating Vedic, Sastra, Purana, and Hindu traditions, as well as instilling Sanatana Dharma ideals, culture, and heritage in future generations.
home.ctaText: This temple was created by the community for the community. Help us continue our mission by providing time, money, or simply by joining and being with us.

trustees.name: Mr. Ram Agarwal
trustees.role: Secretary & In-charge of Temple Religious Services

priests.name: Pandit Pitamber Dutt Sharma
priests.biography: Pandit Pitamber Dutt Sharma is the head priest of the Hindu Temple of Metropolitan Washington. He has been serving the DMV Hindu community for many years, performing Vedic rituals and ceremonies with deep knowledge and devotion.
priests.specialties: Vedic Rituals, Havan, Puja, Life Ceremonies (Sanskar), Katha & Pravachan

priests.name: Shri Ram Pandey
priests.biography: Shri Ram Pandey is a devoted priest serving at the Hindu Temple of Metropolitan Washington. He performs a wide range of Vedic ceremonies and religious services for the community with dedication and spiritual depth.
priests.specialties: Vedic Ceremonies, Pujas, Abhishekam, Wedding & Life Ceremonies
